concerns.text: FR-004 and SC-002 require calculating 'confidence interval coverage rates' (proportion of intervals containing the true value). T034 only mentions calculating 'empirical Type I error rates'. There is no task explicitly covering the aggregation or calculation of CI coverage metrics, leaving a core success criterion unimplemented.

SC-002 requires measuring CI coverage against a known population parameter. T030 explicitly describes implementing 'Sample Mean Deviation' (mapping to SC-003) and 'effect size bias'. No task is tagged or described as calculating the frequency of CIs containing the true value (CI coverage).

The Spec Assumptions state that listwise deletion is used to distinguish 'power loss' from 'Type I error inflation'. T031 implements the deletion but lacks a corresponding task to explicitly measure and report the resulting reduction in statistical power (sample size loss) as a distinct metric, risking conflation of effects.

FR-002 and Constitution Principle VI require injecting three error types (replacement, misclassification, MCAR) at specific rates. T005 defines the schema with rates but does not explicitly mandate that these rates must be applied to *all three* error types, creating ambiguity where a developer might only apply them to one type.

The Plan and Spec require validating the 'clean' estimation procedure across a 'diverse set of datasets'. T013 generates a synthetic grid with only 6 parameter combinations (3 effect sizes × 2 variances). This limited grid may be insufficient to robustly validate the baseline across the full diversity required by the spec.
